programmes.show_description: Place Your Bets! Andrew and Mish hunt for coins in Victoria's Golden Triangle. Michael and Max encounter one of the world's deadliest marine creatures.
Witchcraft In The Devils Triangle. The Witchcraft is one of the strangest disappearances from the Bermuda Triangle. An expedition leads to a shocking discovery on the sea floor.
Ghost Ship Terror. Sightings of ghost ships in the Bermuda Triangle have been reported for centuries. Experts and scientists use cutting-edge technology to try and solve the mystery.
Discover how rally cars, pork pies, floating fountains and artificial stone ornaments are made.
Discover how everyday items such as cufflinks, blueberry turnovers, dashboards and earthenware pottery are made.
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production. How are objects including shuffleboard tables made?
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production. How are objects including air hockey tables made?

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Simon O'Brien transforms some broken pots into a beautiful water feature. Plus, Henry Cole works on a battered motorcycle and a tired side table.

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Max McMurdo and Simon O'Brien travel across the UK to find unique items lost in people's homes and fix them up for a profit. They turn trash into treasure!

programmes.show_name: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programmes.show_description: Max McMurdo and Simon O'Brien travel across the UK to find unique items lost in people's homes and fix them up for a profit. They turn trash into treasure!
Catherine Southon and James Braxton begin their hunt for hidden gems in Eversley. Plus, Catherine learns how a popular flower was saved from extinction.
Catherine Southon and James Braxton hunt for interesting antiques in Evesham. Plus, James uncovers a fascinating tale at the Royal Shakespeare Company.
Catherine Southon and James Braxton travel through Leicestershire in their 1969 Rover. James buys a matchbox holder, while Catherine admires a mirror.
James Braxton and Catherine Southon search for unique antiques in Cromford. Catherine spends big on two carved figures, while James buys a bowl of marbles.
Antiques experts James Braxton and Catherine Southon embark on the last leg of their journey. Who will be victorious at the final auction in Bourne?
First up, Dominic is challenged with reviving a precious wooden toy caravan. Plus, silversmith Brenton restores a unique and very special pair of platinum records.
Silversmith Brenton restores a musical salt and pepper pot from 1955 Hong Kong. Plus, mechanical expert Steve brings a broken childhood doll back to life.
Upholsterer Sonnaz restores a well-loved rocking chair from 1851. Plus, musical restorer David works on a precious antique music box in the shape of a bear.
Angus and the team clear out a big house in Whixall, Shropshire. They uncover a Victorian desk set, a hanging lantern and Vogue clothing patterns.
The Beaver & A Russian Egg. Angus uncovers expensive oak and a Fabergé egg in Herefordshire, while rare single malt whiskey means big bids and spirited surprises!
Singer Russell Watson invites Angus to his amazing home, and ex-Emmerdale starlet Roxy Shahidi invites her co-star Matthew Wolfenden to auction.
Ceramics expert Kirsten is challenged with piecing together a broken clay sculpture. Plus, Will and Suzie work together to fix up a precious childhood toy.
The team get to work on a battered cornet, a distinctive sculpture by an Argentinian artist, and a desk used by an NHS nurse when he was a student.
First up, Dominic is challenged with reviving a precious wooden toy caravan. Plus, silversmith Brenton restores a unique and very special pair of platinum records.
Silversmith Brenton restores a musical salt and pepper pot from 1955 Hong Kong. Plus, mechanical expert Steve brings a broken childhood doll back to life.
Upholsterer Sonnaz restores a well-loved rocking chair from 1851. Plus, musical restorer David works on a precious antique music box in the shape of a bear.
Around the world, there are extraordinary building mistakes. Design teams and experts analyse what went wrong and how engineers are fixing them.
See a Naval pilot as he bails out of a crippled plane moments before crashing, two men who outrun a destructive mudslide and a tornado that sends cars flying!
A modified drag boat almost costs the driver his life, two storm chasers are trapped by a tornado, a military plane spectacularly crashes and a famous bridge disintegrates.
See how a confusing intersection results in a train crash, a picnic turns deadly when a stealth bomber crashes nearby and an eight story building collapses in Manila.
An earthquake completely wipes out a church; a Delta 2 rocket explodes showering Cape Canaveral with flaming debris and a tornado destroys buildings in Oklahoma.
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production. How are objects such as dry erase boards and air rifles made?
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production. How are objects such as squeeze chutes and composite boat propellers made?
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
The team get to work on a battered cornet, a distinctive sculpture by an Argentinian artist, and a desk used by an NHS nurse when he was a student.
Drew and Tee travel to four extraordinary locations across Northern Italy, including the country's most famed antique markets and a dreamy Tuscan villa.
Drew and Tee go hunting for antiques in the homes of fellow dealers. Tucked away in the Cotswolds, Drew finds an ancient Greek masterpiece from 100BC.
Rick's start to the season only gets worse when he finds his truck's been ransacked. Plus, the Dirt Dogs ambush a gold thief and their chances of a profit takes a hit.
The Gold Gypsies get to work to reach their ambitious target. Plus, desperate to get back on track, Rick takes a gamble and heads to an old mining town. But will it pay off?
Roo Irvine and David Harper hunt for antiques in Lincolnshire where they find chocolate moulds and opera glasses. Plus, Roo takes refuge from shopping in a nuclear bunker.
Angus and the team clear out a farmhouse in Cumbria. They uncover some wonderful antiques, including a box of collectable toy cars and a 19th century bureau.
The team get to work on a battered cornet, a distinctive sculpture by an Argentinian artist, and a desk used by an NHS nurse when he was a student.
Herefordshire. Henry and Sam are in Herefordshire where they find sheds full of engines and autogyros, weird and wonderful vehicles before discovering a British classic.
Gloucestershire. The team is in Gloucestershire to uncover some oily treasure. They find a picturesque farm where deer, goats and pigs mingle with vintage agricultural equipment.
Louis. Henry and Fuzz head to Northamptonshire to look around Louis' palatial shed which is filled with motorbikes and some tempting automobilia.
Graham. Henry and Fuzz visit Graham's collection of Land Rovers, motorbikes, and tractors in rural Shropshire. What will Henry and Fuzz take home to restore?
Time Is Money. A huge haul of designer watches sets pulses racing on auction day. Angus helps a woman sell some antiques her late father meticulously restored.
Hope For Glory. A Statue of Liberty, an engine order telegraph and a broken fruit machine go under Angus's gavel as he helps a couple slim their wacky collection.
Reunited After 7 Years. Catherine Southon and Phil Serrell motor through the West Country in their red Morgan Roadster. Who will come out on top in this first leg?
Mystery Treen. Catherine Southon and Phil Serrell go treasure hunting in Dorset, Wiltshire and Hampshire. They find a motoring vanity set and an antique bookcase.
Stuart. Henry and Fuzz find a Triumph Terrier in Oxfordshire. Can they breathe life into this little legend and get it back on the road?
Chris. In Cambridgeshire, Henry and Fuzz uncover a Suzuki GSXR Slingshot. Can they tame this superbike and bring it roaring back to life?
Golden Finish. Guz wraps up his S2000 project, but not without a few wallet-draining surprises. He meets Mani's wild Capri and Bryn's racing Mini before the reveal.
Dara Ó Briain hosts more laughter filled game play with guests Rhys James, Angela Barnes, Sarah Keyworth, Glenn Moore, Milton Jones and Ahir Shah.
How do they make the finest Panama hats? Who rounds up Florida's escaped pythons? How do they keep drivers safe in the world's longest tunnel?
How do they build working miniature airports? How do they design houses to survive hurricanes? How is rhubarb cultivated in the dark?
